author: vicky469
description: Side comments for humans and agents. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: vicky
manifest.description: Side comments for humans and agents.
manifest.name: Aside

name: Aside

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# 2.0.78

## What's new

- **More reliable agent replies**: Aside now persists agent replies more defensively, including streamed replies and sidebar updates, so completed agent output is less likely to be lost or left in a transient state.
- **Clearer agent run context**: Agent cards can show run metadata, and the index opens more predictably when reviewing generated agent activity.
- **Claude runtime compatibility**: Claude runs now receive the expected allowed tool list, and skill usage metadata is extracted from actual tool calls instead of the session-wide skill list.

## Fixes

- **Empty Aside view opens correctly**: Opening Aside without a current markdown note now initializes the sidebar view instead of silently failing.
- **Pasted clipboard blobs stay compact**: Large pasted payloads and long URLs are shortened into cleaner comment text while preserving useful destinations.
- **Cleaner settings surface**: The extra runtime re-check control was removed from settings to keep the agent status area focused.

## Notes

- There are no note-format or vault-data migrations in this release.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/aside/compare/2.0.77...2.0.78

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# 2.0.77

## What's new

- **Nested anchored comments**: Anchored comments can now be moved under another thread while keeping their source selection highlights intact. Moving an anchor into a thread removes the duplicate top-level card instead of leaving the original behind.
- **Quieter workflow feedback**: Routine action notices are suppressed, including missing/orphan note warnings that are already represented visually in the sidebar.
- **Compact agent status**: The settings UI is slimmer, with the previous preferred runtime setting removed and agent status text reduced to the compact inline form.

## Fixes

- **Local HTML links open inside Obsidian**: `.html` and `.htm` links mentioned in comments now open as Obsidian tabs when the file exists in the vault, instead of launching the default browser.
- **Pinned file navigation stays pinned**: Clicking a comment card while the sidebar is pinned to a markdown file selects the card without switching the active editor file.
- **Nested selection highlights remain visible**: Moving selected-text comments into another thread no longer drops the purple source highlight or changes the anchor text rendering.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/aside/compare/2.0.76...2.0.77

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# 2.0.76

## What's new

- **Todo tab default**: The index sidebar now opens on the Todo tab when `@todo` comments exist in the vault. The tab is disabled (not just faded) when there are no todos, and falls back to List automatically.
- **Agent tab removed from index sidebar**: The Agent tab is no longer shown in the index sidebar mode control.

## Fixes

- **Tag extraction**: Tags starting with digits (e.g. `#128`, `#42`) are no longer extracted. Matches Obsidian's rule that tags must start with a letter or underscore — prevents agent-generated text like "Build #128 / PR #42" from producing spurious tags in the index.
- **Todo tab interactive from cached list view**: Switching from Todo → List → Todo no longer gets stuck. The cached list render now computes live thread group counts so the Todo tab stays clickable.
- **Thought trail tab properly disabled**: The Thought Trail tab is now disabled when there are no actual connections to show (wikilinks in comments or shared-tag matches with other vault files). Previously it could appear enabled with an empty view.
- **Index sidebar scope**: All-comments index view now correctly scopes threads vault-wide when no file filter is selected, enabling Todo and Thought Trail to work without requiring a file selection first.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/aside/compare/2.0.75...2.0.76

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release adds Claude CLI support for the agent, introduces a sticky file pin in the sidebar, and includes a run metadata footer for agent threads.

## Highlights

- Agent threads now support the Claude CLI as a backend in addition to the existing options.
- A metadata footer is shown at the bottom of agent run entries so you can see run details at a glance.
- Sidebar panels now support a sticky file pin, letting you lock a specific file into view regardless of editor navigation.

## Notes

- There are no note-format or vault-data migrations in this release.
- The shipped plugin assets remain `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css`.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/aside/compare/2.0.74...2.0.75

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release makes it easier to detach a side note from text without deleting the note itself.

## Highlights

- Selecting text that already has an anchored side note now changes the editor menu action to `Remove anchor from side note`.
- Removing the anchor keeps the note content intact and marks the thread as orphaned instead of creating a duplicate side note.

## Notes

- There are no note-format or vault-data migrations in this release.
- The shipped plugin assets remain `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css`.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/aside/compare/2.0.73...2.0.74

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release makes drafting side notes feel more like writing normal markdown, while tightening source-note navigation from the index sidebar.

## Highlights

- Rich HTML paste in side-note drafts now converts through Obsidian's markdown converter instead of dropping useful structure from copied content.
- Draft editors now continue markdown lists when pressing Enter inside list items.
- Revealing page notes from the index sidebar preserves the source context instead of forcing an unnecessary scroll to the top of the note.
- The sidebar keeps its last supported note context when focus briefly moves to unsupported files.

## Notes

- There are no note-format or vault-data migrations in this release.
- The shipped plugin assets remain `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css`.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/aside/compare/2.0.72...2.0.73

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release improves how built-in agent replies understand and update Aside threads.

## Highlights

- Built-in `@codex` side-note requests now receive page-level context for page notes, so replies can account for the full current markdown page instead of only a nearby section.
- Aside's agent prompt now describes the built-in write workflow more explicitly, including page notes, side notes, side comments, and threaded child entries.
- Regenerating an agent reply now clears the previous reply before the new response completes, avoiding stale text while the retry is running.
- Streaming regenerated replies keep the thread footer focused on agent status instead of showing unrelated footer actions mid-stream.

## Notes

- There are no note-format or vault-data migrations in this release.
- The shipped plugin assets remain `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css`.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/aside/compare/2.0.71...2.0.72

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release makes `Aside index.md` more useful and quieter to scan when comments include tags.

## Highlights

- `Aside index.md` now shows each file's side-note tags inline after the file name, so sidebar comment tags can be explored from the generated index.
- Index file rows now have a little more vertical spacing, making dense file lists easier to read.
- Regular index file names now use the theme's normal text color instead of the purple link color, while the selected file keeps its accent highlight.

## Notes

- There are no note-format or vault-data migrations in this release.
- The shipped plugin assets remain `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css`.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/aside/compare/2.0.70...2.0.71

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release makes the generated Aside index calmer and more reliable, especially while switching files, deleting folders, and loading comments from synced storage.

## Highlights

- `Aside index.md` now ignores empty-space clicks in generated index rows and in the blank preview surface, preventing the full-page flash caused by Obsidian preview rerenders.
- The selected file in the index sidebar now follows the current file immediately instead of lagging behind the previous selection.
- Startup index hydration now uses persisted side-note cache and synced snapshots directly, avoiding unnecessary source-note reads.
- Deleted folders no longer surface missing sidecar cache files as uncaught `ENOENT` errors.
- Legacy inline `<!-- Aside comments -->` migration code is isolated from the normal storage path, while old inline blocks and legacy SideNote2 links remain import-compatible.

## Notes

- No manual migration is required. Old inline comment blocks are still imported and stripped automatically when encountered.
- The shipped plugin assets remain `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css`.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/aside/compare/2.0.69...2.0.70

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release tightens Aside's Codex skill handling and makes deleted-note comment cleanup more durable across synced state.

## Highlights

- Aside refreshes an already installed Codex skill on desktop startup, but no longer creates the Aside skill from a legacy SideNote2 skill.
- Deleted markdown files now write a synced delete tombstone when Aside has known comments from sidecars, open state, aggregate state, snapshots, or pending sync events.
- Added regression coverage for deleting a note whose comments only exist in synced plugin-data snapshots.

## Notes

- Install the Aside Codex skill separately when using external headless workflows. Aside keeps an installed skill current, but does not install or remove it for you.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/aside/compare/2.0.68...2.0.69

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release addresses Obsidian CSS lint warnings from the 2.0.67 release assets.

## Highlights

- Removed broad `!important` overrides from the shipped stylesheet.
- Removed selectors and properties flagged by Obsidian CSS lint, including `:has`, `all`, `column-gap`, `text-decoration-thickness`, `text-decoration-color`, and `mjx-container` type selectors.
- Updated CSS style checks to keep the toolbar and index styles covered without relying on forbidden `!important` rules.
- Updated release-facing README and version bump automation to use marketplace release wording instead of pre-marketplace wording.

## Notes

- The shipped plugin assets remain `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css`.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/aside/compare/2.0.67...2.0.68

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release includes the pending local changes that were staged after 2.0.66, with runtime hardening for sidebar rendering, Thought Trail diagrams, and release artifacts.

## Highlights

- Sidebar, comment highlight, and agent stream rendering now use owner-document DOM checks so UI interactions are safer across Obsidian windows and embedded document contexts.
- Thought Trail Mermaid rendering now resolves either Obsidian's loaded Mermaid runtime or the active window runtime, with extra tests for renderer fallback behavior.
- The build no longer needs the `builtin-modules` package and now uses Node's built-in module list directly.
- Release automation now generates artifact attestations for `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css`.

## Notes

- The shipped plugin assets remain `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css`.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/aside/compare/2.0.66...2.0.67

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release fixes Thought Trail tab rendering for linked-file scopes and removes a slow refresh path when switching sidebar tabs.

## Highlights

- Thought Trail now includes incoming linked files when the current note is only reached through another note's links.
- Switching between List, Tags, and Thought Trail reuses loaded sidebar data instead of reparsing the current note.
- Thought Trail availability and render diagnostics now include scoped file and graph counts to make future tab issues easier to verify.

## Notes

- The shipped plugin assets remain `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css`.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/aside/compare/2.0.65...2.0.66

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release completes the public rename from SideNote2 to Aside and polishes the generated index selection state.

## Highlights

- The plugin now ships as `Aside` with plugin id `aside`, including renamed settings, commands, generated index paths, helper scripts, and Codex skill references.
- Legacy SideNote2 data paths and comment links remain recognized so existing side-note data can continue migrating into the current Aside storage flow.
- The selected file in `Aside index.md` now uses a tighter inline accent highlight instead of a full row highlight with a left strip.

## Notes

- Because the plugin id changed from `side-note2` to `aside`, this release installs into `.obsidian/plugins/aside`.
- The shipped plugin assets are still `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css`.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/aside/compare/2.0.64...2.0.65

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release improves index readability and tightens SideNote2's comment storage internals.

## Highlights

- File names in `SideNote2 index.md` now render larger, making the generated index easier to scan.
- Comment storage now has clearer canonical-storage planning, which keeps legacy block migration and sidecar-backed comment loading easier to reason about.
- Comment lookups now use dedicated indexes, reducing repeated scans when resolving threads and nested entries.

## Notes

- There are no note-format or vault-data migrations in this release.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.63...2.0.64

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release clarifies SideNote2 empty-state guidance in the sidebar.

## Highlights

- The index sidebar empty state now points users to click a file in the index to filter the sidebar and inspect that file's side notes.
- The note sidebar empty state now explains both ways to start: use the add button for a page side note, or select text and right-click "Add comment to selection" for an anchored note.

## Notes

- There are no note-format or vault-data migrations in this release.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.62...2.0.63

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release tightens comment editing behavior and sidebar tab state styling.

## Highlights

- Comment editor formatting now handles bold and highlight operations more consistently for multiline and list-item selections.
- Comment draft dismissal is less aggressive when interacting with sidebar comments, so normal sidebar clicks do not unexpectedly exit the active edit flow.
- Sidebar tab states now follow Obsidian theme text colors, avoid hardcoded black, and make disabled tabs visibly faded.
- Project philosophy documentation has been refreshed.

## Notes

- There are no note-format or vault-data migrations in this release.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.61...2.0.62

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release updates the bundled `sidenote2` Codex skill for structured page-note workflows.

## Highlights

- The bundled `sidenote2` skill now treats "one point a note/comment" as one parent SideNote2 thread with each point appended as a child comment, instead of creating separate page-note threads.
- Adds a repo helper for creating a page-note thread with ordered child comments, which keeps bulk note import and repair workflows aligned with SideNote2's threaded storage model.
- Helper-created side notes now refresh both note-path and source-id sidecar storage, so renamed-note recovery and synced source identities stay aligned.

## Notes

- Users who already installed the external `sidenote2` Codex skill will get the updated skill content automatically when this plugin release starts on desktop.
- SideNote2 still does not silently install a new external Codex skill for users who have not opted into that workflow. Built-in `@codex` side notes, including desktop local runtime use, do not require a separate skill install.
- There are no note-format or vault-data migrations in this release.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.60...2.0.61

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release refines the index sidebar card workflow and makes deleted-note review safer.

## Highlights

- Adds read-only index sidebar tag filtering while keeping empty tag views empty instead of falling back to all comments.
- Makes index sidebar cards use the same selected-text preview treatment as individual note sidebars.
- Opens nested thread details by default and keeps deleted parent threads visible with their child entries.
- Restricts index cards to view/filter/resolve behavior by removing edit, add, delete, share, move, and retry actions.
- Adds per-card restore and permanent-delete controls in deleted mode, replacing the bulk permanent-delete toolbar action.
- Improves mobile index file clicks by revealing the sidebar when a file filter is selected.
- Tightens sidebar tab, toolbar icon, no-file empty-state, and thought-trail availability behavior.

## Notes

- The release artifact check covers `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` for source maps, embedded sources, local paths, and common secret patterns.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.59...2.0.60

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release rebuilds the index sidebar around file-level navigation and hardens Thought Trail rendering.

## Highlights

- Simplifies `SideNote2 index.md` to a folder/file list while keeping the header image and caption styling stable.
- Lets index file links filter the SideNote2 sidebar instead of jumping directly to source notes.
- Adds index sidebar List and Thought Trail modes with compact file filtering, resolved visibility, delete actions, and source-note redirects from thread cards.
- Keeps the selected index sidebar tab when changing file filters instead of always returning to List.
- Fixes duplicate sidebar rendering caused by overlapping async index renders.
- Fixes Thought Trail Mermaid rendering by removing custom Obsidian click links from direct Mermaid input and cleaning markdown links from edge labels.

## Notes

- Thought Trail nodes remain clickable through SideNote2's own DOM binding; edge labels are display text only and do not redirect.
- The release artifact check covers `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` for source maps, embedded sources, local paths, and common secret patterns.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.58...2.0.59

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release hardens SideNote2 sync across devices and makes the generated index easier to use.

## Highlights

- Preserves mobile and desktop replies under the same thread when synced plugin data arrives out of order or from compacted snapshots.
- Adds source-note identity recovery so renamed markdown files can keep their sidebars without stealing notes from same-name files in other folders.
- Speeds up visible-sidebar sync polling so mobile changes appear faster on desktop for the currently focused notes.
- Makes generated index file rows collapsible with smaller `h5` headings, cleaner file labels, and safer native fold-button handling.
- Fixes mobile card controls so tapping side-note cards stays inside the side-note panel instead of navigating the workspace unexpectedly.

## Notes

- Source sidebars now use stable source IDs with path-hash fallback for older cache data.
- Ambiguous legacy recoveries are intentionally skipped instead of mutating the wrong file's sidebar.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.56...2.0.58

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release cleans up remaining legacy hidden SideNote2 blocks and hardens plugin-data sync for Obsidian Sync users.

## Highlights

- Recognizes old object-shaped `<!-- SideNote2 comments -->` blocks, migrates any missing thread data into canonical storage, and strips the block from source markdown.
- Re-runs storage and sync migrations so vaults that already passed earlier migrations still get cleaned up.
- Merges SideNote2 sync state with the latest plugin data before writing, reducing the chance that two active synced devices overwrite each other's event logs.
- Keeps sidecar files as rebuildable local cache; synced plugin data remains the cross-device source when Obsidian Sync is enabled for plugin data.

## Notes

- Users who want cross-device SideNote2 sync should enable Obsidian Sync for plugin data.
- Source markdown no longer needs hidden SideNote2 data after migration.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.55...2.0.56

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release keeps batch-applied tags formatted inline so side notes stay compact and readable.

## Highlights

- Fix batch tag appends so newly applied tags stay on the same line instead of being split across separate lines.
- Keep duplicate tag suppression and local note scoping unchanged.

## Notes

- This is a small patch release for the note-sidebar tag workflow.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.54...2.0.55

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release fixes an editor jump after revealing a side note, adds a little more breathing room under the note toolbar, and makes GitHub release reruns safe when the same version already exists.

## Highlights

- Defer revealed-selection cleanup until after outside editor clicks settle so normal note clicks no longer jump back near the previously revealed anchor.
- Add extra bottom padding under the note toolbar in tags mode to keep the batch tag controls from feeling cramped against the content below.
- Make the release workflow idempotent when a GitHub release already exists for the pushed tag.
- Update release metadata and re-upload `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` on reruns instead of failing at `gh release create`.
- Keep the normal first-run release flow unchanged for brand-new version tags.

## Notes

- The jump fix affects local note clicks after a comment was previously revealed and you scrolled away from it.
- This release also includes maintenance for release automation, but it does not change the remote bridge feature set or note storage format.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.53...2.0.54

releases.author: vicky469
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release expands local tag workflows in the note sidebar and improves responsiveness across sidebar and source-note editing.

## Highlights

- Add local tag browsing and batch tag actions in the note sidebar, including cleaner batch-state reset behavior.
- Speed up sidebar edit, save, delete, and resolve flows by avoiding redundant local data refreshes and reducing thread signature work.
- Improve source markdown responsiveness by making editor highlight range calculation cheaper during typing and selection changes.
- Tighten the tag-tab toolbar layout so controls remain visible in narrower sidebars and search fields no longer crowd out buttons.

## Notes

- This release ships as `2.0.53` because `2.0.52` already exists as a GitHub tag/release.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.52...2.0.53

releases.author: vicky469
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release restores the note sidebar’s Thought Trail header controls so users can switch back to list mode after opening Thought Trail.

## Highlights

- Ensure the individual note sidebar always renders its toolbar in Thought Trail mode, including list/thought-trail tab controls.
- Keep the note sidebar header visible for the mode switch, preventing users from being stuck in Thought Trail.
- Syncs correctly after rebuilding and installing the updated bundle.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release moves SideNote2 canonical storage from inline hidden JSON blocks to per-note sidecar files, solving the large-note rewrite problem and making the plugin more reliable.

## Highlights

- **Per-note sidecar storage**: Side-note thread data now lives in `.obsidian/plugins/side-note2/sidenotes/by-note/<hash-prefix>/<full-hash>.json` instead of a trailing `<!-- SideNote2 comments -->` block inside each markdown file.
- **Automatic migration**: On first startup after updating, the plugin scans all notes, migrates legacy inline blocks into sidecars, and strips the old managed blocks from source notes. No manual action required.
- **Zero source-note mutation after migration**: Once migrated, the plugin never rewrites the source note for comment operations. Only the small sidecar file is updated.
- **Support modal improvements**: Added an "Open data folder" button to the support inspector that opens the sidecar directory in your system file manager.

## Notes

- This release includes a one-time automatic migration. Vaults with many large notes will see a brief startup delay while blocks are converted.
- If a single note fails to migrate, the legacy block is left untouched and retried later.
- Repo-side helper scripts (`comment:create`, `comment:append`, etc.) now write to sidecars instead of inline blocks.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.50...2.0.51

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release tightens the draft-save flow for anchored notes, simplifies more of the sidebar chrome, and makes side-note navigation and note-link creation more reliable.

## Highlights

- Empty anchored side notes can now be added directly from the sidebar and collapse back to preview after save, while empty page-note drafts stay blocked until you enter content.
- Sidebar cards are simpler and more stable: pin state now persists, bookmark support has been removed, the preview expand affordance is visible again, and the meta/action layout is cleaner.
- Opening side-note deep links is more reliable, and creating linked notes from comments now sanitizes unsafe or invalid paths before file creation.

## Notes

- This release removes bookmark-specific sidebar behavior that was previously exposed in the UI.
- No SideNote2 thread-format migration is required.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.49...2.0.50

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release simplifies the sidebar, removes the experimental side-note-to-side-note linking flow, and tightens up a few performance and stability edges around rendering and hidden comment blocks.

## Highlights

- Sidebar cards are simpler to scan and use: pin and bookmark controls now live in the header, and comment open interactions have been simplified.
- The experimental side-note reference linking flow has been removed. `Link side note`, `Mentioned` / `Mentioned by`, the link-state indicator, and local note-chain Thought Trail sections are gone. Thought Trail is back to file-level relationships only.
- Sidebar and editor behavior are more stable: highlight work during markdown scrolling has been reduced, hidden SideNote2 comment blocks behave correctly outside source mode, and agent regenerate fallback/disabled-state behavior has been restored and simplified.
- The note-sidebar export feature has been removed.

## Notes

- This release does not introduce a new stored SideNote2 thread format.
- Legacy trailing `Mentioned:` side-note reference blocks are treated as old data and stripped on future SideNote2 parse/write paths.
- For existing notes you want to clean in bulk, use:
  `node scripts/strip-side-note-links.mjs --path "<note-or-folder>"`


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.48...2.0.49

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release makes the sidebar easier to scan and makes moving side notes feel more direct, both across files and within a thread.

## Highlights

- Selected-text previews in the sidebar are now clamped to two lines, which keeps more context visible without letting each card take over the list.
- The index sidebar now has its own side-note search, so you can filter and rank side notes directly from the index view instead of falling back to the note-local sidebar.
- `Move side note` now targets markdown files directly, opens the destination note after the move while reusing an existing tab when possible, and nested child notes can now be re-parented locally with the same drag handle used for page-note ordering.

## Notes

- This release does not change the stored SideNote2 thread format. The move-flow updates stay within the existing note-backed data model.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.47...2.0.48

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release fixes index-note navigation again and makes agent reply insertion target an open markdown file instead of forcing the source note.

## Highlights

- Clicking file headings and comment rows in `SideNote2 index.md` now reliably scopes the right sidebar again, without the sticky multi-selection behavior that could show up after repeated clicks.
- Agent reply comments now offer `Add to file`, which lets you choose from currently open markdown files and append the cleaned reply body there.
- Restoring deleted comments is more reliable for both root comments and child entries, and editor highlights now survive more anchor-normalization cases.

## Notes

- `Add to file` works with open markdown files only. If a target file is in preview mode, SideNote2 will switch that leaf into editing mode before appending to the end.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.46...2.0.47

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release tightens agent runtime recovery, trims deleted mode down to the actual deleted cards, and removes the old repo-local SideNote2 CLI path in favor of the bundled skill installer flow.

## Highlights

- Remote agent runs now fail fast when they stall instead of lingering indefinitely, and index pin state stays scoped so temporary pin focus does not leak across index views.
- Deleted mode in the note sidebar now shows only the soft-deleted cards themselves. Deleted child entries no longer force the whole live thread to stay visible.
- The repo-local SideNote2 CLI entrypoint has been removed from the development workflow. The bundled skill installer script now owns that setup path instead.

## Notes

- This release does not change the shipped plugin surface for SideNote2 thread data. The CLI cleanup only affects repo and Codex setup paths.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.45...2.0.46

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release makes sidebar pinning durable again and cleans up the optional external Codex skill setup docs.

## Highlights

- Sidebar pins now persist per file in the saved SideNote2 view state, so both the pin markers on comment cards and the pinned-only toolbar view come back when you return to that note.
- The README now treats the public `sidenote2` Codex skill as optional advanced setup and points external Codex users at the GitHub `$skill-installer` flow instead of implying a separate local SideNote2 CLI install.
- The remote bridge base URL setting copy now explicitly allows either HTTP or HTTPS for localhost and private LAN development.

## Notes

- Pin state is persisted as per-user sidebar workspace state, not inside the markdown note itself. Bookmarks remain the note-backed long-lived state.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.44...2.0.45

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release makes sidebar pinning more explicit and keeps the optional external `sidenote2` Codex skill aligned automatically for users who already installed it.

## Highlights

- Pinned side notes no longer silently narrow the sidebar list on their own. Instead, SideNote2 now keeps pinning as a saved selection and gives you an explicit pinned-only toggle when you want to focus on that subset.
- If you already installed the external `sidenote2` Codex skill for handoff workflows, SideNote2 now refreshes that installed skill automatically when a newer plugin release starts up.
- The bundled `sidenote2` skill guidance now distinguishes normal CLI/chat explanations from in-thread SideNote2 write-back flows, which prevents pasted thread URIs from being treated as automatic reply instructions.

## Notes

- There are no note-format or vault-data migrations in this release.
- The automatic skill refresh only applies when the external `sidenote2` skill is already installed; built-in `@codex` side notes still do not require any separate skill install.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.43...2.0.44

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release makes built-in `@codex` threads behave more predictably while adding an explicit runtime picker for local and remote agent execution.

## Highlights

- Same-thread `@codex` replies no longer wait for the previous run in that thread to finish before starting; SideNote2 now starts them up to the normal runtime concurrency limit and keeps each reply directly after the question that triggered it.
- Agent Runtime settings now let you explicitly pick `Local` or `Remote`, with inline availability status shown beside each option in the native Obsidian settings style.
- Runtime labels are now consistently shown as `Local` across the settings and status UI.

## Notes

- There are no note-format or vault-data migrations in this release.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.40...2.0.43

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release makes note drafting feel calmer and faster by reducing the sidebar flash when opening a new side note, while also giving the note toolbar a darker sidedock-style surface.

## Highlights

- Opening a new anchored or page-note draft now skips the extra pre-focus sidebar rerender, so the textarea appears faster with less visual flash.
- Draft focus still lands the cursor in the textarea automatically, but avoids the temporary active-highlight treatment that made the toolbar and thread area feel jumpy.
- New note-sidebars use a darker sticky toolbar surface that better matches Obsidian's sidedock chrome.

## Notes

- Draft selection hashes are now materialized lazily at save time; no note data migration is required.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.41...2.0.42

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release adds note-scoped markdown export and tightens the note sidebar interactions so export and thread controls stay out of the way until you need them.

## Highlights

- Added a note-sidebar export action that writes the current file's side notes to `SideNote2/exports` as clean markdown.
- Exported markdown keeps anchored selections as thread labels, preserves connected side-note references as wiki links, and strips noisy `Mentioned:` / side-note URI blocks.
- Export links now use alias-style wiki paths when needed, so duplicate file names stay unambiguous while the visible link text stays minimal.
- The note sidebar toolbar is cleaner: `Thought Trail` hides the search and add-note row, and nested thread toggles appear only after you single-click a comment card.

## Notes

- Exports are stored as flat files under `SideNote2/exports`, and re-exporting updates the existing file for that source note.
- No note-format or vault-data migration is required for this release.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.40...2.0.41

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release adds the cross-platform runtime bridge work from `feature/cross-platform-runtime-dgx`, so SideNote2 can prefer your local desktop Codex setup when it is available and use a developer-managed remote bridge when it is not.

## Highlights

- SideNote2 can now route `@codex` runs through a remote runtime bridge, which enables agent workflows on devices where the local desktop runtime is unavailable.
- Auto runtime selection now prefers the local desktop runtime on supported filesystem desktops and falls back to the configured remote bridge for other environments.
- Settings now expose advanced remote-bridge URL and token fields, a connection test button, and clearer runtime availability messaging.
- Mobile sidebar draft editing has a tighter keyboard and button layout, which makes the compose flow more usable on smaller screens.

## Notes

- Remote bridge support is intended for developer-managed setups and requires a configured base URL plus bearer token.
- SideNote2 requires HTTPS for remote bridge endpoints, except for localhost or private-LAN HTTP during development.
- There are no note-format or vault-data migrations in this release.


## What's Changed
* Feature/cross platform runtime dgx by @vicky469 in https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/pull/2

## New Contributors
* @vicky469 made their first contribution in https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/pull/2

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.39...2.0.40

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release tightens the preview-card header layout and smooths out new-note saving in the note sidebar.

## Highlights

- Comment card datetimes now sit on the far right of the header by default, which makes the preview metadata line read more cleanly.
- Header action buttons no longer reserve width while a card is inactive, so the metadata shifts left only when the card becomes active and the actions appear.
- New anchored notes now save with a lighter path that skips the pre-save sidebar rerender, defers the aggregate refresh, and avoids the extra persist-time sidebar refresh.
- Bookmark quick-save keeps that lighter path and still skips anchor revalidation, so both normal Add and bookmark save feel more responsive.
- Threads with nested replies now stay expanded while the parent thread is active, even when the global nested-comments toggle is set to hide replies, so active `@codex` exchanges do not collapse immediately after autosave returns to preview.

## Notes

- There are no note-format or vault-data migrations in this release.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.38...2.0.39

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release makes comment markdown in the sidebar behave more like native Obsidian markdown.

## Highlights

- Sidebar comment previews now keep loose list paragraphs attached to their bullet items more reliably, so multi-paragraph bullets render with the expected structure.
- Rendered comment markdown no longer applies the custom `pre-wrap` override that was making list layout diverge from native Obsidian markdown rendering.
- Markdown-render normalization now includes regression coverage for bullet items with follow-up paragraphs and for headings that should stay outside the list.

## Notes

- There are no note-format or vault-data migrations in this release.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.37...2.0.38

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release makes the note sidebar easier to scan and easier to use as a lightweight bookmark surface.

## Highlights

- The note sidebar now supports both a list view and a thought-trail view rooted on the current file, so you can switch between flat comment scanning and local graph navigation without leaving the note.
- Bookmarked threads now have a first-class bookmark state, a bookmark filter in the note sidebar, and a consistent bookmark marker on saved cards and draft controls.
- Bookmarked notes can now save without body text, and active comment cards keep a stable accent frame instead of flashing through a background-color transition while you add or save notes.

## Notes

- There are no note-format or vault-data migrations in this release.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.36...2.0.37

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release fixes a sidebar regression around nested comment toggles.

## Highlights

- Threads with nested replies now always keep their show or hide icon visible, even after you turn nested comments off from the top toolbar.
- The top nested-comments control now acts as the default for all threads instead of removing each thread's local toggle.
- Individual threads can still be expanded or collapsed after the global nested-comments control changes.

## Notes

- There are no note-format or vault-data migrations in this release.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.35...2.0.36

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release smooths out sidebar card interactions so editing, controls, and source reveals feel more predictable.

## Highlights

- Comment action buttons now stay hidden until a card is explicitly selected, instead of appearing on hover or scroll.
- Threads with nested replies keep their show or hide toggle visible at all times, so it remains obvious where to click.
- Inline editing no longer steals the caret when you reposition it inside the draft textarea.
- Revealing an anchored side note now scrolls to the actual stored text span, which fixes long-line notes where the selected text could still be off-screen.

## Notes

- There are no note-format or vault-data migrations in this release.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.34...2.0.35

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release tightens how sidebar comment cards open so the index view stays local and predictable.

## Highlights

- Double clicking a sidebar comment card now opens the comment target instead of starting edit mode.
- In a normal note sidebar, double click still jumps to the source selection in the source note.
- In `SideNote2 index.md`, double click now reveals the matching index-note preview instead of redirecting to the source file, since the explicit redirect icon already handles source jumps.

## Notes

- Single click on a comment card now just marks it active in the sidebar.
- There are no note-format or vault-data migrations in this release.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.33...2.0.34

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release makes threaded side-note editing feel lighter in the sidebar and adds finer control over long reply chains.

## Highlights

- Parent side-note cards can now hide or show only their own nested replies, so long threads are easier to collapse without changing the rest of the note.
- Thread entry edits now stay inline inside the existing card, with smoother save behavior and outside-click autosave for sidebar interactions.
- Sidebar action chrome stays more stable while hovering and editing, which reduces card jumpiness during reply management.

## Notes

- The settings tab no longer shows the built-in Codex status panel.
- There are no note-format or vault-data migrations in this release.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.32...2.0.33

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release makes threaded side-note conversations behave more predictably while keeping comment editing faster from the sidebar.

## Highlights

- Agent replies triggered from a child comment now land directly after that child instead of falling to the end of the thread.
- Persisted side-note cards can now enter edit mode with a double click, so revising a reply is faster from the sidebar.
- Double-click edit ignores deleted cards and interactive targets, so normal buttons and links keep their existing behavior.

## Notes

- The page-note toolbar still uses the top-right `Add page note` action.
- Child comment ordering in existing threads is preserved; only new generated replies now insert closer to the comment that triggered them.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.31...2.0.32

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release tightens the sidebar interaction model so page-note ordering stays predictable and the newest streamed Codex replies remain editable as soon as they finish.

## Highlights

- The sidebar now keeps index ordering and draft placement stable, with top-level drafts inserted in their natural position instead of jumping to the end.
- Child-note `+` actions now open the append draft directly after the clicked child entry, while the parent `+` still appends at the end of the thread.
- Manual drag reordering is now limited to page notes, which removes the more fragile thread-entry reorder path from the sidebar UI.
- Newly finished Codex replies no longer lose their edit-button click handlers after the stream handoff, so the latest reply can be edited immediately.
- `@codex` side-note replies now explicitly prefer compact ASCII diagrams and avoid Mermaid in the sidebar.

## Notes

- Existing Mermaid text already stored in notes is not rewritten automatically; the ASCII guidance applies to new side-note replies.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.30...2.0.31

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release removes SideNote2's PDF-note support and cleans up the sidebar so unsupported files no longer inherit stale note-local UI.

## Highlights

- Side notes are now markdown-only. PDF note support and the old attachment-comment storage path have been removed from the plugin and docs.
- The note-local sidebar now clears correctly on unsupported files, so switching to a PDF no longer leaves the previous markdown page-note cards visible.
- Sidebar target resolution now treats an unsupported active file as no sidebar target instead of falling back to the last supported note.

## Notes

- Existing SideNote2 interactions continue to work in markdown notes and in the SideNote2 index note.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.29...2.0.30

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release makes SideNote2's agent experience clearer during long Codex runs and reduces visual churn in the note sidebar.

## Highlights

- `@codex` requests now pack smarter default context from the current note, section or anchor, nearby headings, and recent thread transcript instead of relying on broad note dumps.
- Live agent replies now show real progress text from Codex when available, with a simplified footer that focuses on the spinner and progress instead of extra status labels.
- Cancelling an active agent run now stops the work while keeping the reply card in place, so the thread does not disappear mid-run.
- The note-level sidebar now reuses existing thread cards during refreshes, which reduces the flashing and full-card remounting that used to happen when comments were added or updated.

## Notes

- The `obsidian://side-note2-comment?...` protocol is still a navigation target for the UI; it is not yet expanded into agent prompt context automatically.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.28...2.0.29

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release makes the sidebar easier to scan by surfacing the note context first and shrinking timestamp noise.

## Highlights

- Anchored side notes in the note sidebar now show the selected text before the timestamp, with ellipsis when the preview is too long.
- Index sidebar rows now lead with the most useful context: page notes show the page name, while anchored notes show the selected text.
- Sidebar timestamps now use a more compact Obsidian-style format such as `1:30 PM`, `Yesterday`, `Fri 1:30 PM`, or `Mar 10`.
- Persisted sidebar cards no longer react visually on hover while scrolling; the neutral active state and action icons now appear when the card is selected.

## Notes

- These changes are presentation-only. They do not change comment storage or thread data.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.26...2.0.28

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release makes persisted side note cards quieter by default so the display stays focused on the note itself.

## Highlights

- Hid comment action icons, author labels, and footer chrome by default on hover-capable devices, then revealed them on hover or active focus.
- Removed the `You` author badge from user-written persisted comments to cut visual noise.
- Kept agent reply author labels visible when needed and preserved touch-device usability by leaving controls visible on non-hover devices.

## Notes

- This change only affects persisted display cards. Edit mode and streaming cards keep their existing behavior.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.25...2.0.26

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release adds a faster deleted-comments cleanup flow in the page sidebar and keeps the deleted view scoped to the current note.

## Highlights

- Added `Empty trash` in the page sidebar deleted view to permanently clear deleted side notes for the current source note.
- Removed the extra confirmation prompt for `Empty trash` so deleted-view cleanup is one step.
- Kept the deleted-comments cleanup logic out of the index sidebar and covered the file-scoped purge flow with new tests.

## Notes

- `Empty trash` only appears in the page sidebar while deleted view is active.
- The action clears deleted threads and deleted child entries for the current source note, then returns the sidebar to the normal view.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.24...2.0.25

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release tightens the comment lifecycle in the sidebar and removes stale deleted-file threads from the index view.

## Highlights

- Added page-sidebar soft delete with restore, deleted-only filter, and retention-based cleanup.
- Tightened agent reply regeneration so retries stay attached to the same reply card instead of creating duplicate cards.
- Fixed the index sidebar so comments from deleted source files no longer linger after the source note is removed.

## Notes

- The deleted-comments flow is page-sidebar only. The index sidebar continues to hide deleted threads from the aggregate view.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.23...2.0.24

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release tightens the index toolbar layout so the mode tabs and filter controls fit more cleanly in the sidebar.

## Highlights

- Made the index mode tabs more compact and rebalanced their width so `List`, `Thought Trail`, and `Agent` use the row more cleanly.
- Switched the `Files` and `Resolved` toolbar filters to icon-only controls so the toolbar matches the rest of the row and wastes less space.
- Reduced extra padding in the tab control so the sidebar toolbar stays denser without changing the overall visual style.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.22...2.0.23

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release adds built-in `@codex` side notes on desktop Obsidian. Save a side note with `@codex`, watch the reply stream in the sidebar, and review agent threads from a dedicated Agent tab without needing the external SideNote2 CLI or skill for the built-in flow.

## Highlights

- Added built-in `@codex` dispatch for side notes on desktop Obsidian, with SideNote2 appending the final reply back into the thread.
- Added streaming run state, persisted agent history, retry support, and a dedicated Agent tab with success and failure filters in the index sidebar.
- Simplified onboarding around built-in `@codex`, including a read-only Codex status check in settings and updated docs and product copy.

## Notes

- Built-in `@codex` requires desktop Obsidian with a filesystem-backed vault and a working local Codex installation.
- External share-link handoff still exists for advanced workflows, but built-in `@codex` is now the primary path.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.21...2.0.22

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release hardens SideNote2 note storage so a markdown note must have exactly one managed `<!-- SideNote2 comments -->` block, and it refuses further writes when duplicate managed blocks are present.

## Highlights

- SideNote2 now rejects notes that contain more than one managed `<!-- SideNote2 comments -->` block instead of silently treating only the last block as managed.
- The CLI now reports a specific duplicate-block error so it is clear when a note must be repaired before creating, appending, updating, or resolving comments.
- Managed-block detection now uses a single shared analyzer for parsing, visibility, ranges, and write validation, which removes repeated rescans of the same note content.
- Added regression coverage for duplicate managed blocks in both storage helpers and the `comment:create` CLI path.

## Notes

- If a note already contains duplicate SideNote2 managed blocks, collapse it back to exactly one block before writing new comments.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.20...2.0.21

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release makes pasted long URLs readable inside SideNote2 comments by storing them as shortened markdown links while preserving the full destination.

## Highlights

- Long bare URLs pasted into new or edited side notes are now saved as shortened markdown links, so stored comments stay compact without breaking the underlying link target.
- Existing comments that still contain raw long URLs now render with shortened link labels in the sidebar, even before they are re-saved.
- Page-note previews in `SideNote2 index.md` now show clean link labels instead of raw markdown-link syntax.

## Notes

- Existing stored raw URLs are rewritten into the new shortened markdown-link form the next time that comment is edited and saved.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.19...2.0.20

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release adds first-class CLI support for creating SideNote2 page notes and anchored notes in an existing markdown file, and clarifies the agent workflow so those requests default to simple in-note threads instead of separate wiki pages.

## Highlights

- Added `sidenote2 comment:create` so agents and users can create a new page note or anchored note directly in an existing note file.
- Updated the bundled SideNote2 guidance so phrases like `page note` and `anchored note` map to an in-note SideNote2 thread unless the user explicitly asks for a separate wiki page.
- Added regression coverage for page-note creation, anchored-note creation, and the legacy flat-comment rejection path.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.18...2.0.19

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release fixes wiki-link handling for nested SideNote2 thread replies so linked notes stay connected even when the link lives in an older child comment instead of the latest entry.

## Highlights

- Fixed derived comment links so SideNote2 now scans every entry in a thread when building wiki-link metadata and backlinks.
- Fixed the index file filter graph and Thought Trail view so thread connections from child replies are preserved instead of disappearing after newer replies are added.
- Added regression coverage for derived-link metadata, index graph construction, and Thought Trail rendering for multi-entry threads.

## Notes

- There are no note-format or vault-data migrations in this release.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.17...2.0.18

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release fixes SideNote2's open-note sync behavior when the note-backed comment block changes on disk outside the editor.

## Highlights

- Fixed external SideNote2 comment updates for already-open notes so managed-block changes can appear without closing and reopening the note.
- Added regression coverage for the external managed-block sync path and for the distinction between open editor content and stored file content.
- Refined the comment persistence controller to reuse the visible note body while refreshing comment state, derived links, editor decorations, and markdown previews.

## Notes

- This is not a CRDT merge layer. The auto-sync path only applies when the visible note body is unchanged and the difference is limited to the managed `<!-- SideNote2 comments -->` block.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.16...2.0.17

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release fixes a Thought Trail rendering regression and tightens the plugin against the current Obsidian review checks.

## Highlights

- Restored Thought Trail Mermaid styling so connected-file graphs render correctly instead of collapsing into black shapes.
- Switched the repo to the official Obsidian ESLint plugin and cleaned up the remaining source issues around deprecated APIs, DOM rendering, and async overrides.
- Hardened several sidebar and support surfaces while keeping build, lint, and test coverage green.

## Notes

- There are no note-format or vault-data migrations in this release.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.15...2.0.16

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release tightens the inline draft editor so adding a side note stays compact and typing-focused in narrow desktop sidebars.

## Highlights

- Removed the explicit draft textarea label that was surfacing as a distracting accessibility popup during customer-facing add-comment flows.
- Fixed the focused draft editor so typing uses the real textarea layer instead of a misaligned overlay preview.
- Reduced the default add/append draft height and removed the narrow-screen textarea inflation that made placeholders and typed text appear oversized.

## Notes

- There are no note-format or vault-data migrations in this release.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.14...2.0.15

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release removes the dev-only diagnostics blocker from the mobile compatibility path.

## Highlights

- Removed the top-level Node `path` dependency from plugin startup so SideNote2 can load without forcing a desktop-only manifest.
- Kept the local log inspector and `Locate log` behavior runtime-gated to local desktop environments instead of blocking mobile installs.
- Updated the submission checklist to reflect the real remaining task: a manual Android or iOS smoke test before the marketplace PR.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.13...2.0.14

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Highlights

This release tightens SideNote2's release safety and rendering hardening as part of marketplace submission prep.

## Highlights

- Added an automated shipped-artifact inspection step that blocks source-map markers, embedded sources, obvious secrets, and local-only paths from public releases.
- Hardened comment rendering paths and added regression coverage so HTML-like input stays inert in the draft preview.
- Simplified the public GitHub bug report flow.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/compare/2.0.12...2.0.13

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# SideNote2 2.0.12

## Highlights

- Adds required versioned release notes for every release, with GitHub release pages now combining manual highlights with generated change notes.
- Moves long-lived repo documentation into `docs/` and promotes `README-dev.md` to the repo root for a clearer contributor entrypoint.
- Standardizes the agent write path around the installable `sidenote2` CLI instead of repo-local script paths.

## Notes

- The plugin manifest now includes the Buy Me a Coffee funding link.
- There are no note-format or vault-data migrations in this release.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vicky469/SideNote2/commits/2.0.12

releases.author: vicky469
releases.description: Beta release 2.0.11.
